canonical_name: a garden filed with scentless flowers and eternal peace
the forest where the little folk live still
the grove where I lay with the young stag in the darkness of the eclipse
the maiden who had sent forth the young stag to challenge the running deer
the eternal One who summoned me forth to life
his pall, and for Arthur who hated me now, and for Elaine who had been my friend
that shoreless sea of which I may not speak
a mother she has not seen for many years
the house where once Viviane had dwelt
the child who had not lived long enough to be born
the daughter I had never known
some who had seen the tree of the Holy Thom
the priestesses in the House of Maidens
Maidens whose breasts had scarcely formed
the very holy places of Avalon
the orchards white with blossom in the moonlight
no true priestess, chosen of the Goddess
the channel which overflowed from the Well to make this still pool
Morgaine could hear the voice of Taliesin
that God who would drive out all other Gods
the instrument of the traitor's punishment
all those who dwelt on Avalon
the woman who shows no fear or revulsion of him
that woman who shows him again the manhood he craves and fears
all those before me have lain in the enchanted country
